title: Generation of mouse bone marrow-derived dendritic cells (BM-DCs)

steps: Flush mouse tibiae and femurs with ice-cold PBS through a 70 μm-wide cut-off cell strainer.
Centrifuge 5’ at 1400 rpm. Resuspend pelleted cells in conditioned medium (supplemented with 10% of growth supernatant of GM-CSF-transduced B16 cells).
Seed 7 x 10 6 cells in 100 x 20 mm non-treated cell culture plates in 10 ml of conditioned medium.
On day 4 and 7 add 5 ml of pre-warmed conditioned medium.
At day 8/9 the percentage of CD11c+ cells should be higher than 90% as measured by FACS analysis. BMDCs are then ready for experimental use.
Harvest the supernatant and gently wash the plate once with 5 ml of pre-warmed PBS.
Incubate 2’ with 5 ml of 2mM EDTA at 37 °C – 5% CO 2 .
Collect cells, wash once with PBS.
Centrifuge 5’ at 1200 rpm and resuspend pelleted cells in conditioned medium.

CD14 regulates the dendritic cell life cycle after LPS exposure through NFAT activation by Ivan Zanoni, Renato Ostuni, Giusy Capuano, Maddalena Collini, Michele Caccia, Antonella Ellena Ronchi, Marcella Rocchetti, Francesca Mingozzi, Maria Foti, Giuseppe Chirico, Barbara Costa, Antonio Zaza, Paola Ricciardi-Castagnoli & Francesca Granucci published in Nature on 15 May, 2009 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature08118
